Inflation Signal
← Back to dashboard

Inflation Signal

Broad U.S. inflation signal from price-level dynamics.

As of 2026-03-31

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Given an inflation_signal z-score of 0.0007 and a regime classification of COOL, this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Indeterminate volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not trend continuation (1).
  • The signal reflects a state of disinflationary impulse, currently transitioning from Neutral into a COOL regime (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Deteriorating; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • A composite z-score between -0.25 and +0.25 defines a TRANSITION state, signaling a structural turning point (1).
  • The COOL regime is defined by a z-score ≤ -0.50, though the current data labels values near zero as COOL (1).
  • Economic momentum is assessed by comparing 3-month annualized CPI against the YoY CPI rate (1).
  • Inflation Signal (latest observation: 2026-03-31) (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the rapid compression of the inflation_signal z-score toward zero, falling from 0.347 to 0.0007 in one month (1).
  • Falling values imply a disinflationary slowdown or potential for expansionary policy pivot (1).
  • Internal Conflict: The current z-score sits in the "TRANSITION" band (-0.25 to +0.25), yet the provided regime label identifies it as COOL (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A 3-month annualized CPI exceeding YoY CPI would signal accelerating momentum and invalidate the disinflationary bias (1).
  • The signal shows deteriorating stability as it approaches a regime-flip threshold.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Persistent transition state leading to a confirmed disinflationary regime.
  • Upside risk: Sharp reversal in 3-month momentum triggered by unexpected supply-chain or energy shocks.
  • Downside risk: Acceleration into a deep COOL regime triggered by a cyclical demand collapse.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) as the signal tracks 10-year rolling trends and monthly momentum (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; the transition-zone position necessitates confirmation from liquidity or copper signals (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs aggregate inflation pressure, influencing policy expectations and macro risk budgeting (1).
  • Economic mechanism: Disinflationary impulse or demand deceleration, which typically serves as a tailwind for fixed income (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with M2 liquidity and copper prices; divergence from these would suggest cost-push rather than demand-pull dynamics (1).

Regime Context:

  • Newly entered COOL regime (per provided label), following a period of Neutral stability (1).
  • Direction of change: Strengthening disinflationary momentum.

Model Limitations:

  • Subject to CPI revisions, seasonality, and news-based noise from Economic Policy Uncertainty (EPU) (1).
  • TIPS liquidity bias during stress may distort market-implied components (1).

Data & References:

  • US Inflation Signal (2026-03-31) (1).
  • Influential data: Latest z-score of 0.000778 (1).
  • Additional data: M2 Money Supply and Copper Prices would improve interpretation confidence (1).
